name: Community Housing Innovations, Inc.
description_short: Non-profit housing organization providing emergency shelter, eviction prevention, and financial literacy counseling across NY counties. Mission-driven support from crisis to homeownership.
description_long:
Community Housing Innovations (CHI) is a non-profit organization dedicated to breaking the cycle of poverty through comprehensive housing and support services across New York. The organization serves families and individuals experiencing housing instability, homelessness, and financial hardship in Westchester, Dutchess, and Long Island counties. CHI operates as a mission-driven entity focused on moving families "from crisis to permanence" through integrated housing and supportive services.

CHI offers a range of housing solutions matched to different stages of housing insecurity: temporary/emergency housing for families in acute crisis; supportive and permanent housing with wraparound services for individuals with special needs; a Rental Supplement Program (RSP) to stabilize families; eviction prevention with emergency assistance and advocacy; foreclosure prevention counseling; and financial literacy education. Support services are designed to address root causes of housing instability beyond shelter alone, including job support and financial skills building. The organization also provides first-time homebuyer orientation and fair housing education.

CHI distinguishes itself through its holistic, wraparound service model that combines housing placement with financial counseling and life skills training. The organization has established partnerships with corporate and community sponsors and maintains an active calendar of free educational events (homebuyer orientations, fair housing workshops, financial literacy sessions) open to the public. Their approach explicitly targets breaking poverty cycles rather than temporary relief alone, with documented outcomes tracking households placed into permanent housing.

As a legitimate non-profit, CHI provides genuinely free services—there are no loan products, fees, or credit-based barriers to accessing help. The main limitation is geographic scope: services are concentrated in three NY counties (Westchester, Dutchess, Long Island), making them unavailable to consumers outside these regions. Website metrics (household/individual served counts) display as "0+" without actual figures, limiting transparency around service volume and outcomes.
pros: Completely free services with no fees, loans, or credit requirements
Comprehensive wraparound approach combining housing with financial literacy and job support
Multiple housing solutions for different crisis levels (emergency, temporary, permanent, supportive)
Free public events including first-time homebuyer orientations and fair housing workshops
Eviction prevention and foreclosure prevention counseling provided at no cost
Established partnerships with corporate sponsors and gala fundraising showing community backing
Serves highest-need populations including those with special needs and chronic homelessness
cons: Services limited to three NY counties (Westchester, Dutchess, Long Island) with no multi-state reach
Website lacks specific outcome metrics—household/individual served numbers display as '0+' without actual data
No clear eligibility criteria published on website (income limits, family size, documentation requirements)
Limited information about wait times, application process, or how to access services beyond general 'Contact Details' links
No online application or intake process described; requires contacting organization directly
best_for: Families facing immediate eviction or homelessness in Westchester, Dutchess, or Long Island counties
First-time homebuyers seeking free education and guidance in NY region
Homeowners in foreclosure in Westchester or Dutchess County needing prevention counseling
Individuals seeking free financial literacy and budgeting education to build long-term stability
diagnosis: CHI is best for NY residents (Westchester, Dutchess, Long Island) experiencing housing instability, homelessness, eviction risk, or foreclosure who need free, comprehensive support combining shelter with financial education and job services. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—services are unavailable outside three counties, and interested consumers should verify eligibility and current service availability by contacting CHI directly.
services: Temporary/emergency housing assistance for families in crisis
Supportive and permanent housing with wraparound services
Rental Supplement Program (RSP) to reduce homelessness
Eviction prevention with emergency financial assistance
Foreclosure prevention counseling (Westchester and Dutchess counties)
Financial literacy education and financial counseling
Fair housing and tenant rights education
Case management and wraparound support services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 55 Medford Ave., Ste. B, , Patchogue, NY 11772
